avoid.market: Will the Fed cut rates 0 times? (KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0)
avoid.reason: High volume and tight spread but no independent edge vs market; cannot ground 2026 Fed path without current FOMC projections, inflation trajectory, or labor data releases beyond supplied prices

avoid.market: Will the Federal Reserve cut rates before 2027? (KXRATECUT-26DEC31)
avoid.reason: Implied ~26.5% consistent with 0-cut market after definition differences (Jan vs Feb start); no credible specific catalyst or source for material divergence

avoid.market: Will the Fed cut rates 1 times? (KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T1)
avoid.reason: Low standalone edge; residual probability after 0-cut market leaves little room and no independent forecast source available

avoid.market: Will the WTI crude oil settlement price be above 77.99 USD/Bbl on Jul 17, 2026? (KXWTIW-26JUL1714-T77.99)
avoid.reason: Passes volume/spread filter but no current WTI futures price, inventory data, or EIA report accessible; cannot form grounded prob without fabricating source

avoid.market: Will there be a recession in 2027? (KXRECSSNBER-27)
avoid.reason: 41.5% mid is plausible for distant NBER-window risk but historical base rates and GDP markets do not yield >=8% edge with specific grounding source

avoid.market: All weather (NYC/Chicago/Miami high temp Jul 16), gold, Brent, copper, ETH, silver, CPI, GDP, hurricane markets
avoid.reason: Fail volume < $50k filter and/or lack real-time forecast/price data required for non-fabricated grounding; short-dated commodity and weather contracts especially untradeable under rules

notes: Neutral regime: market pricing of zero 2026 cuts (~79%) coexists with moderate Q2 GDP expectations (~1.5-2.5% range from lower-volume markets) and subdued July CPI prints. Strict filters (volume >=50k, spread <=0.10, category edge thresholds, mandatory non-fabricated grounding source, no external data) eliminate all candidates. Zero trades is the correct output; no edge stretching or invented sources.
